prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "C-Skip 跑姿练习" (C-skip running drill) exercise, side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style. Person posture: Photographic freeze-frame at the SIGNATURE 'CYCLING-DOWN' phase of a C-skip drill (the bottom-back arc of the cycling motion). Side view facing right. The right foot is on the ball-of-foot in ground contact. The LEFT LEG is the active drill leg in mid-cycle: the left thigh has descended from horizontal back down toward the standing position (now angled about 30-45 degrees from vertical, going down), AND the left knee is BENT so the left lower leg (shin) curls BACKWARD with the left FOOT TUCKED BEHIND-AND-BELOW THE KNEE (like the bottom-back portion of a bicycle pedal stroke, tracing a C-shaped arc). The visual signature: an athlete's leg making a circular paddling motion at the BOTTOM of the cycle. Arms in running-style opposition. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: NOT a standing running stride; NOT thigh held high horizontal (that is A-skip); NOT shin extended forward (that is B-skip). The defining feature is the leg mid-DESCENT with shin curled back.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors and quadriceps and hamstring of the cycling leg, plus calf muscles of the supporting leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the hip flexors and quadriceps; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "小腿 / 臀大肌" pointing to the calf and glute area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
